fact: October 27 is exactly 300 days into the year! That means there are only 65 days left until New Year's Eve.
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for squirrels to go absolutely nuts burying acorns - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season!
The sun sets before 6 PM in most northern cities by this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
October 27 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign represented by a creature that glows under black light!
Maple trees are dropping their leaves like crazy right now in temperate regions, creating natural leaf tornadoes when the wind picks up.
Why do spiders seem to be everywhere indoors around this time? They're not actually invading - they're just more visible as they search for warm winter hideouts!
The moon rises about 50 minutes later each night, so if you saw it rise at 7 PM yesterday, tonight it'll pop up around 7:50 PM.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, October 27 is prime springtime - flowers are blooming and baby animals are being born everywhere!
Morning frost becomes a real possibility in many northern areas by this date, turning spider webs into glittery masterpieces.
October 27 is roughly 35 days after the autumn equinox, meaning daylight has been shrinking by about 2-3 minutes every single day in the north.
Geese flying south create their famous V-formations most actively around this time - the lead goose works 20% harder than the others!
Apple trees have finished dropping their fruit by now, leaving behind a sweet, fermenting mess that makes wasps act drunk and sluggish.
